accurate.why: All claims track the bill text and accurate context (Do Not Pay under title 31, SSA, clear-and-convincing evidence, notification of errors) is correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims; references to title 31 and the Social Security Act align with the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill improves death-data sharing between SSA and the Do Not Pay system to prevent and recover improper payments.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the bill amends the Social Security Act and references the title 31 Do Not Pay system.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures key conditions: 'to the extent feasible,' clear-and-convincing-evidence requirement, and cooperative-arrangement requirements.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the amendments take effect December 27, 2026.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers data sharing, cost-sharing agreement, evidentiary standard for recording deaths, and error-notification requirement.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent across provisions.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Commissioner of Social Security, SSA, and the Do Not Pay working system under title 31.

correct_figures.why: The only figure—the December 27, 2026 effective date—is correct.
